name: sign-guard
description: PreToolUse(Write|Edit|NotebookEdit|Bash) sign-gate for governance-SSOT files, implemented in hexa-lang (`_sign_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). A small registry (GATED) of high-stakes files тАФ currently `commons.tape` (cross-project do/dont layer), `project.tape` (per-project identity + governance), and `.gitignore` (commit-hygiene: silently controls what enters version control, so a stealth agent edit could un-ignore a secret or hide a file from review) тАФ may be edited by the agent ONLY while a fresh user sign-off token exists. The user mints one with `sidecar sign <key>` (`commons` ┬╖ `project` ┬╖ `gitignore`), which writes the current epoch to `~/.sidecar/signs/<key>.sign`; a token is valid for 300s (5 min). Covers both write channels (mirrors hexa-native): structured file tools (Write/Edit/NotebookEdit) and the Bash redirect channel (`> X`, `>> X`, heredoc, `tee X`, `dd of=X`, `cp`/`mv` dest; relative targets resolved against `cwd`). Writes buried inside an invoked CLI (e.g. `sidecar init`'s own redirect) are not visible at the top-level command string, so the official scaffolder is unaffected тАФ only the agent's own direct writes gate. The hook CODE-ENFORCES the no-self-mint rule: it hard-denies the agent's mint command `sidecar sign <key>` (list / clear stay allowed) AND any write forging a token under `~/.sidecar/signs/`. Because the agent's tool calls (Bash ┬╖ Write/Edit) route through this hook, the agent CANNOT self-mint. Mint detection (0.1.5) is narrowed to a real command INVOCATION: the Bash channel is scanned on a QUOTE-MASKED copy of the command and `sidecar` must sit at a command position (first token or right after a shell separator `;`┬╖`&&`┬╖`||`┬╖`|`┬╖`&`), so a `sidecar sign <key>` MENTION inside a quoted commit message (`git commit -m 'тАж'`) or PR body (`gh pr --body 'тАж'`) is no longer mis-flagged as a self-mint тАФ pre-0.1.5 it was, wrongly blocking legitimate commits that merely referenced the command. The `bin/sidecar` /dev/tty layer remains the backstop against deliberate quote-evasion. (The interactive `!` bang a USER types in the TUI prompt does NOT route through the hook and mints freely тАФ the intended user path, safe because the agent cannot emit a `!` bang.) `bin/sidecar` carries an OPT-IN /dev/tty second layer (`SIDECAR_SIGN_REQUIRE_TTY=1`) that defeats hook-parser-evading obfuscation. The fresh token is the sole precondition (project.tape @D s11: gate on a real precondition, never an opt-out switch); enforcement is the hard deny (project.tape @D s13). To gate another file: add one `["key", "/suffix"]` row to GATED and reship. NO env-var / flag override by design.
